{"step_by_step_thinking": "The C wave in JVP corresponds to the tricuspid valve closure during ventricular systole. This closure creates a positive pressure wave that can be observed in the jugular venous pulse. Among the given options, tricuspid bulging (option C) is the correct description of the C wave. Atrial contraction (option A) is associated with the A wave, atrial filling (option B) is associated with the V wave, and rapid ventricular filling (option D) is not directly related to the C wave.", "answer_choice": "C"}
